intro: Oryzopsis kingii, also known as King's rice grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of China and the Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oryzopsis kingii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make tea, decoctions, and infusions, and is also used in the production of herbal tablets and capsules.
